The driver who crashed into a prep school killing two girls has been charged with causing death by dangerous driving nearly three years on from the tragedy. Nuria Sajjad and Selena Lau, both eight, were killed when a Land Rover Defender veered off the road smashing through a fence into the Study Prep School in Wimbledon as children and staff were at an end-of-year tea party in July 2023. Nine children and three adults were also seriously injured.

This article is a little bit misleading. This poor guy had been unknowingly suffering from a never-ending cycle of topical steroid addiction for years. It looks very much like worsening eczema but it is actually the skin requiring stronger and stronger potencies of steroids over time. Although diet will undoubtedly help eczema, the biggest improvement is due to the process of topical steroid withdrawal and allowing the skin to heal from the damage caused by steroids. I’ve been through the whole horrific process.

The mother looks fantastic

Ten years ago, there was nothing I dreaded more than a shopping trip with my mother. I was 15, she was 51, and our tastes differed wildly. I would sit scrolling on my iPhone while she deliberated over a camel blazer in Karen Millen, unenthused by the rails of clothes. If I was lucky, I could persuade her to let me have a quick look in River Island before we headed back to the car park - then it was her turn to feel bored as I tried on a silly cropped vest top. The sequinned and skimpy pieces on offer were, unsurprisingly, not to her taste.
  • 23 comments
  • share The trendy brands fashion editor, 25, and her mum, 61, love

President Donald Trump praised the 'special relationship' between the US and UK in his welcome speech, while King Charles spoke about its historic importance in his address to Congress. But it was Queen Camilla and First Lady Melania Trump who really put on a show of togetherness - especially through their wardrobes. During a state visit where photo ops were just as important as the words being said, the two wives proved that sartorial choices could make or break the picture of unity. Thankfully, Camilla, 78, and Melania, 56, showed they were firmly on the same page, often mirroring each others style and subtly paying tribute to one another.

Fans have been left heartbroken following the news of Gone Fishing's terrier Ted death earlier this week.  The Patterdale Terrier mix had passed away at the age of 13, having cemented himself as a key part of BBC's Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing. Nice weather to be travelling across the English Channel today

Parts of Britain could be hotter than Hawaii today as Britain enjoys its warmest day of the year so far while forecasters also warned of possible heavy rain and thunder. Temperatures could reach 27C (81F) in London and East Anglia at the end of the working week - but changeable weather is on the way for the bank holiday weekend. Such a high would make today the UK's warmest day of 2026 so far - more than three weeks after the existing record of 26.6C (79.9F) was set in London on April 8.

Don’t try and mix football and politics, leave them to it.

FIFA president Gianni Infantino failed in an effort to get the football chiefs of Israel and Palestine to shake hands on stage in a tense moment at the governing body's Congress in Vancouver. Infantino had invited the leadership of both federations to speak at the Congress amid the ongoing conflict, with each addressing delegates for several minutes. After Israel FA vice president Basim Sheikh Sulima had spoken second, Infantino requested he remain on the stage.

  • I lived in ETA ("East Twickenham Actually") just across the river back in the mid-1970's. It was a delightful place with its Ice Rink, coffee bar where the older teenagers and early twenty somethings hung out. Sitting on a wall by the river on a sunny evening, I recall a part being in full swing in the house behind. Roger Daltrey (The Who) popped his head through the gatewayl and asked "What are you doing sitting there? Come on in a join our party! It's my birthday, come in and have a proper drink!" Would that sort of thing happen today? I doubt it!

  • I'm 77. don't feel invisible because I'm not. It's a state of mind. I see my fellow women & watch the grey mist descend. It pervades everything. It's almost like they feel they need permission to exist. Of course you're invisible! But you don't have to be. Society doesn't expect it. Don't let yourself fade away girls! Do new things. A South African removal driver had a miracle escape after the brakes of his pick-up truck failed, sending it plunging over a cliff. Dramatic CCTV footage shows Courage Ncube, 34, heading down the steep road in his loaded Toyota with two colleagues standing in the back when suddenly he couldn't stop. The brake pedal in his truck just slammed straight down to the floor as the Toyota suddenly sped forward at a terrifying rate towards a tight 45-degree right hand bend.

I would not pay £1.4m to live in East Belfast- except perhaps Ormiston House.

The property in Northern Ireland has been owned by the same family for decades and is full of period features, offering its next owner a sense of grandeur. Lynzi Templeton, a manger at John Minnis Estate Agents in Belfast, which is selling the property told This is Money: 'Given its scale and flexibility, the property is likely to appeal to owner-occupiers seeking a prominent, spacious premises in a well-connected area, or investors and developers attracted by the size and potential.'

  • share Belfast home complete with butler's pantry goes on the market
